summary: McKenzie reports that his Bingo Card Creator software has reached the first page of Google search results for its target keyword, ranking #8 through his Payloadz download listing. However, he tempers the excitement by noting the listing isn't ideal because the Payloadz web store design is suboptimal. He identifies this as something to optimize but acknowledges limited control over a third-party storefront.
key_points: Bingo Card Creator reached page one of Google for 'bingo card creator' within 11 days of launch
The ranking came through a third-party payment processor (Payloadz) rather than his own site
He recognizes that ranking alone isn't enough — the landing experience matters for conversions
He notes the tradeoff of using third-party platforms: limited control over presentation but beneficial backlinks
money_quotes: If you search "bingo card creator", I'm now result #8 through my download link at Payloadz.
Its not that useful to me, though, because the web store design is not so hot.
key_insight: Ranking on Google's first page matters less than the quality of the page users actually land on.
